title: Agents and Infrastructure, Sam Lambert | Compile 26
summary: Sam Lambert, CEO of PlanetScale, discusses how infrastructure must evolve to support autonomous agents. The talk demonstrates agents optimizing and sharding a real database, addressing branching, safety mechanisms, undoing bad changes, and building infrastructure designed to safely handle agent-driven operations.
writeup: Sam Lambert argues that database and deployment infrastructure needs to be redesigned so AI agents can safely handle optimization and operations tasks, rather than counting on model capabilities alone to prevent mistakes. The talk demonstrates agents automatically sharding databases and makes the case for branching, reversibility, and safety mechanisms as core infrastructure features. The takeaway: as agents take on operational work, the systems they act on must provide guardrails and undo paths by design.
